name: web_scraper
description: Advanced web scraper that extracts clean article text, metadata, structured content, and intelligently scored images from web pages. Handles JavaScript-rendered pages, strips boilerplate navigation and ads, and returns clean readable content. Supports full LLM analysis packaging, smart image filtering by relevance score, RSS feeds, and web search.

action.description: Operation to perform. 'analyze_content' is the recommended action for LLM analysis — it returns a structured package with summary, full text, key images, tables, and a ready-to-use llm_prompt field.

url.description: The URL of the web page to scrape or analyze.

analysis_goal.description: For 'analyze_content': describe what you want to know or do with the page content. This is embedded in the returned llm_prompt. Example: 'Summarize the main findings and extract key statistics.'

extract_mode.description: Extraction mode for scrape_full. 'auto' detects the best mode from the page type.

smart_images.description: Enable intelligent image relevance scoring when downloading. Filters out icons, logos, tracking pixels, and noise. Only returns content-relevant images above min_image_score.

min_image_score.description: Minimum relevance score (0.0–1.0) for images when smart_images is true. Higher = stricter filtering. Default 0.4.

max_images.description: Maximum number of images to process. Default 20.

output.description: File path for the primary output file (e.g., scraped content JSON or llm_prompt.txt). If omitted, defaults to the active task's tools/web/ directory.

query.description: Search query string for the 'search' action.

max_results.description: Maximum search results to return. Default 10.

output_dir.description: Directory to save output files (JSON manifests, downloaded images, llm_prompt.txt). Defaults to the active task's tools/web/ directory when available.

wait_for_js.description: Whether to attempt JavaScript rendering via Selenium before falling back to static fetch. Default true.

wait_timeout.description: Page load timeout in seconds. Default 15.
